\begin{proof}
This is Theorem~\ref{thm:universal-theta}, proved intrinsically by
Theorem~\ref{thm:mc2-bar-intrinsic}: the cyclic
$L_\infty$-algebra $\Defcyc(\cA)$ is encoded by the genus-completed
bar differential, the completed tensor product converges with
clutching by Proposition~\ref{prop:geometric-modular-operadic-mc},
and the scalar trace is genus-$1$ universal with all-genera
$\kappa\lambda_g$ only on the proved uniform-weight lane
(Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality}).
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Scope]\label{rem:universal-MC-scope}
The scalar shadow has universal genus-$1$ coefficient
$\kappa(\cA) \cdot \lambda_1$, and on the proved uniform-weight lane
the full series $\kappa(\cA)\cdot\lambda_g$ is established
(Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality}). The clutching compatibility
is implicit in the modular operad structure of the bar complex
(Theorem~\ref{thm:prism-higher-genus}). At the one-channel level,
Proposition~\ref{prop:tautological-line-support-criterion} shows that
these two geometric inputs would already force tautological-line

\begin{theorem}[Family index theorem for genus expansions;
\ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:family-index}
\index{index theorem!modular deformation|textbf}
\index{Grothendieck--Riemann--Roch!genus expansion|textbf}
Let $\cA$ be a Koszul chiral algebra with obstruction coefficient
$\kappa(\cA)$, let $\pi \colon \overline{\mathcal{C}}_g \to
\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g$ be the universal curve with relative
dualizing sheaf~$\omega_\pi$, and let
$\mathbb{E} = R^0\pi_*\omega_\pi$ be the Hodge bundle.
The following holds for uniform-weight modular Koszul algebras
at all genera; the genus-$1$ statements are unconditional for
all families, including multi-generator.
Define the \emph{modular deformation complex} of~$\cA$ at genus~$g$ by
\begin{equation}\label{eq:modular-deformation-complex-concordance}
\mathcal{D}_{\cA}^{(g)}
\;:=\;
\kappa(\cA) \cdot \mathbb{E}

-Grothendieck--Riemann--Roch refinement
-(Theorem~\ref{thm:family-index}).
+Part~(iv): on the uniform-weight lane the algebraic part of the
+curvature is one-dimensional, so the only $\cA$-dependent scalar is
+$\kappa(\cA)$ from part~(ii). The geometric part is the Hodge
+bundle~$\mathbb{E}$: because every scalar propagator is the
+weight-$1$ form $d\!\log E(z,w)$, no higher Hodge bundle
+$R^0\pi_*\omega_\pi^{\otimes h}$ with $h \neq 1$ enters the scalar
+sector. Let $\Theta_{\mathbb{E}}$ be the Chern curvature matrix of
+$\mathbb{E}$ in the Arakelov metric. Chern--Weil gives
+\[
+\det\!\Bigl(\frac{i}{2\pi}\Theta_{\mathbb{E}}\Bigr)
+\;=\;
+c_g(\mathbb{E})
+\;=\;
+\lambda_g .
+\]
+Applying this Euler-form polynomial to the geometric factor and the
+linear scalar extraction to the algebraic factor yields
+\eqref{eq:obstruction-hodge}. Pairing with $\psi_1^{2g-2}$ and using
+Theorem~\ref{thm:mumford-formula} gives the generating series
+\eqref{eq:family-index-package}.
 \end{proof}

+The singular part of the OPE of $\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}$
+is the sum of the singular parts of $\mathcal{A}$ and
+$\mathcal{B}$, since the tensor factors act on disjoint sets of
+fields. The genus-$1$ curvature is therefore additive, so the
+genus-$1$ clause of Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality} gives
+\[
+\kappa(\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B})
+\;=\;
+\kappa(\mathcal{A}) + \kappa(\mathcal{B}).
+\]
+On the proved scalar lane, applying
+\eqref{eq:genus-universality} to this identity yields
+\[
+\mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B})
+\;=\;
+(\kappa(\mathcal{A}) + \kappa(\mathcal{B})) \cdot \lambda_g
+\;=\;
+\mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{A}) + \mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{B})
+\]
+for all $g \geq 1$.
+\end{proof}

+The singular part of the OPE of $\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}$
+is the sum of the singular parts of $\mathcal{A}$ and
+$\mathcal{B}$, since the tensor factors act on disjoint sets of
+fields. The genus-$1$ curvature is therefore additive, so the
+genus-$1$ clause of Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality} gives
+\[
+\kappa(\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B})
+\;=\;
+\kappa(\mathcal{A}) + \kappa(\mathcal{B}).
+\]
+On the proved scalar lane, applying
+\eqref{eq:genus-universality} to this identity yields
+\[
+\mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B})
+\;=\;
+(\kappa(\mathcal{A}) + \kappa(\mathcal{B})) \cdot \lambda_g
+\;=\;
+\mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{A}) + \mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{B})
+\]
+for all $g \geq 1$.
+\end{proof}

thinking
**Evaluating prose on uniqueness**

I’m contemplating whether to say that supporting prose earlier in the foundations implies there’s no step for clutching uniqueness. It’s possible that it might not be necessary to mention that aspect at all. I'm trying to determine if it adds value or if it might just confuse things. I think it's important to keep the focus clear, so maybe I should lean toward simplicity in this case.
thinking
**Summarizing verification results**

I’ve verified that `git diff --check` passed, and targeted greps found no old phrases in the files. The fast build finished without fatal LaTeX errors, but there are still 102 undefined references. I might mention that it’s not fully clean, but AP225 is closed. I’ll start the report with “FIXED.” It’s unnecessary to state what I didn’t do unless relevant, and I’ll reference key theorem lines and remarks without overwhelming the reader with details.
thinking
**Crafting the final statement**

I need to be careful not to inaccurately state that there are "no remaining old phrases" when it only applies to the target files. Instead, I should say "in the target files." That keeps it clear and precise. Now, I’ll focus on crafting a concise final statement that accurately reflects this detail while ensuring it is straightforward and easy to understand. Clarity is key here!
codex
FIXED. AP225 is closed on disk.

In [higher_genus_foundations.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/higher_genus_foundations.tex:5453), `thm:genus-universality` no longer uses scalar saturation or clutching-uniqueness. The proof now takes the geometric scalar sector to be the Hodge bundle, applies Chern–Weil to its curvature matrix, and derives
`\mathrm{obs}_g(\cA)=\kappa(\cA)\det((i/2\pi)\Theta_{\mathbb{E}})=\kappa(\cA)c_g(\mathbb{E})=\kappa(\cA)\lambda_g`
directly. The downstream GRR packaging in [higher_genus_foundations.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/higher_genus_foundations.tex:6488) and the alternate route in [higher_genus_modular_koszul.tex](/Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/higher_genus_modular_koszul.tex:3037) are aligned to that same Euler-form argument and explicitly reject the clutching-uniqueness step.

Verification: targeted greps in the two target files found none of the old AP225 phrasing, `git diff --check` passed on the target surface, and `make fast` completed without a fatal LaTeX error. The manuscript still has a pre-existing background of 102 undefined references, including existing `thm:family-index` refs, but that is not introduced by this AP225 repair.

+The singular part of the OPE of $\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B}$
+is the sum of the singular parts of $\mathcal{A}$ and
+$\mathcal{B}$, since the tensor factors act on disjoint sets of
+fields. The genus-$1$ curvature is therefore additive, so the
+genus-$1$ clause of Theorem~\ref{thm:genus-universality} gives
+\[
+\kappa(\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B})
+\;=\;
+\kappa(\mathcal{A}) + \kappa(\mathcal{B}).
+\]
+On the proved scalar lane, applying
+\eqref{eq:genus-universality} to this identity yields
+\[
+\mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{A} \otimes \mathcal{B})
+\;=\;
+(\kappa(\mathcal{A}) + \kappa(\mathcal{B})) \cdot \lambda_g
+\;=\;
+\mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{A}) + \mathrm{obs}_g(\mathcal{B})
+\]
+for all $g \geq 1$.
+\end{proof}
